message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker status

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -726,6 +726,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-p3sd","title":"FUSE E2E: fuse_spec_i5_delete_reclaims_free_counts fails at file 169","description":"The test creates 1000 files in a subdirectory then tries to delete them all. File 169 consistently returns ENOENT during fs::remove_file. File 169 is the first entry in the second directory block (the directory transitions from 1 block to 2 blocks at this point).

Root cause analysis:
- Core-level test (create 1000 + lookup + unlink) passes - the filesystem logic is correct
- The failure is FUSE-specific - kernel dentry cache or FUSE protocol interaction
- The MVCC store correctly holds all committed data
- Adding sync/flush between create and delete phases does not fix it
- The failure is 100% deterministic at file 169

Possible causes:
1. FUSE kernel negative dentry caching interfering with create/unlink sequence
2. Directory extent resolution issue specific to the FUSE lookup path under kernel cache pressure
3. Kernel dentry eviction + daemon lookup returning stale data

Next steps: Add FUSE-level tracing/logging to the lookup and unlink paths to capture the exact sequence of FUSE operations around file 169","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T19:06:40.043910040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T19:37:10.917554076Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T19:37:10.917280702Z","close_reason":"Fixed: checksum tail was not initialised in new directory blocks, causing parse_dir_block to fail with rec_len overflow","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-pcfw","title":"Harden ffs-fuse edge-case tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T09:09:37.037112732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T09:14:45.101424849Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T09:14:45.101404972Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-plyn","title":"Bitmap CRC: cross-validate crc32c crate against software implementation","description":"Write a test that computes CRC32C of the same 4096-byte bitmap data using:
(a) the crc32c crate 0.6.8 (current)
(b) a manual software CRC32C implementation (polynomial 0x1EDC6F41, reflected)
(c) the crc32fast crate (if available) for a third opinion

Compare all three results. If they differ, the crc32c crate has a hardware-acceleration bug on this CPU. If they agree, the bug is in our bridge function or the data we're checksumming.

Test data: a real mkfs.ext4 bitmap block (or a synthetic 4096-byte buffer with known CRC).

ACCEPTANCE: A test that definitively proves whether the crc32c crate produces correct results for 4096-byte inputs on this platform.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:11.470459311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:25:26.728288089Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:25:26.727814833Z","close_reason":"Root cause found: bitmap CRC was chaining group_number + using full block length. Fixed in commit 20d65f4.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crc32c","ext4","investigation"]}
+{"id":"bd-pqpu","title":"Implement FIEMAP ioctl passthrough in ffs-fuse","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:54:37.576185576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:54:42.588757333Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:54:42.588475389Z","close_reason":"Implemented FIEMAP ioctl in ffs-fuse with FsOps::fiemap trait method, ext4 extent mapping, FUSE ioctl handler, and 2 unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-pv9l","title":"e2compr: implement double/triple indirect write and cluster freeing","description":"Extend the indirect write support to double-indirect (i_block[13]) and triple-indirect (i_block[14]) levels. This covers files up to the ext4 maximum.

Also implement cascading free: when all pointers in an indirect block become 0, free the block and propagate the zero to the parent level. This prevents block leaks when large compressed files are deleted or truncated.

DEPENDS ON: Single-indirect write support (bd-z15u).

ACCEPTANCE: Compressed write works for arbitrarily large files. Deleting a compressed file frees all indirect blocks.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:18.026009802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:53:04.109030647Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:53:04.108567410Z","close_reason":"Added double-indirect (i_block[13]) and triple-indirect (i_block[14]) write support with lazy intermediate block allocation. Extracted ensure_indirect_block and ensure_pointer_in_block helpers. 490 tests pass. Commit 06a4288.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2compr","ext4","write_path"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pv9l","depends_on_id":"bd-z15u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:28.301559286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-pzc6","title":"Implement i_version increment-on-write for NFSv4 and statx(2) compatibility","description":"BACKGROUND: ext4 inodes have a 64-bit version counter split across two fields: i_version (l_i_version at offset 0x24, low 32 bits) and i_version_hi (offset 0x98, high 32 bits). This counter is used by NFSv4 for cache coherence (the NFS change attribute) and by statx(2) via STATX_CHANGE_COOKIE. The kernel increments it on every inode metadata or data change via ext4_inode_set_iversion_raw().

CURRENT STATE: Commit 4a1fb8a added the version and version_hi fields to Ext4Inode and wired them into parse/serialize, so the values are preserved across read-modify-write cycles. However, we never INCREMENT them — every write-back produces the same version counter the inode had when we read it.

IMPACT: NFS clients mounting a FrankenFS-served ext4 image via NFS will never see version changes, causing them to serve stale cached data indefinitely. The statx(2) STATX_CHANGE_COOKIE field will also be stale.

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
1. Add a helper function bump_inode_version(inode: &mut Ext4Inode) that increments the 64-bit version counter (version + version_hi as a combined u64, then split back).
2. Call bump_inode_version in every write path that modifies the inode: ext4_write, ext4_create, ext4_mkdir, ext4_unlink, ext4_rmdir, ext4_rename, ext4_link, ext4_symlink, ext4_setattr (truncate, chmod, chown, utimes), ext4_setxattr, ext4_removexattr.
3. Only bump when metadata_csum or 64bit features are enabled (the field is only meaningful with extended inodes).
4. Add a test that performs a write, reads back the inode, and verifies version > original_version.
5. Add a test that performs multiple writes and verifies the version is monotonically increasing.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Every inode-modifying operation bumps i_version.
- Version is monotonically increasing within a mount session.
- Round-trip test: write, read-back, verify version increased.
- No version bump on read-only operations (getattr, read, readdir).
- The 64-bit counter wraps correctly at u64::MAX (wrapping_add).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:38.971610895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:23.987199529Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:23.986685286Z","close_reason":"Added bump_inode_version() called from touch_mtime_ctime() and touch_ctime(). All 3,591 tests pass. Commit f3c69f2.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","inode","nfs"]}
 {"id":"bd-q0g","title":"ext4 semantics: Implement permission/uid/gid + mode mapping details [phased]","description":"Future task: ensure getattr reports correct permission bits and ownership, and FUSE enforces them where appropriate.

Deliverables:
- Map inode mode bits to POSIX file types + permissions.
- Surface uid/gid correctly (including high bits).

Acceptance:
- getattr output matches kernel for a fixture inode set.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"PinkCreek","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:47.212225418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:22:20.184893543Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:22:20.184870791Z","close_reason":"Implemented device_number/major/minor on Ext4Inode, wired rdev in inode_to_attr. uid/gid/permissions were already correct. 8 tests added.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-q0g","depends_on_id":"bd-10t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:01.896707087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-q0g","depends_on_id":"bd-3qq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:01.815846651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-v1r","title":"Implement format-aware scrub validators for ext4/btrfs superblocks","description":"Scope:\n- Replace scrub CLI placeholder-only validator stack with format-aware validators.\n- Add ext4 superblock validator (parse + geometry + metadata checksum when enabled).\n- Add btrfs superblock validator (parse + superblock checksum).\n- Keep scrub engine format-agnostic with pluggable validators.\n- Add unit tests for both validators and validator selection in CLI path where practical.\n\nAcceptance:\n- cargo test for affected crates passes.\n- No regressions in existing scrub tests.\n- FEATURE_PARITY/ParityReport updated if capability counts change.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:20:52.577227565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:31:12.099229919Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:31:12.099209722Z","close_reason":"Implemented ext4+btrfs superblock scrub validators, wired CLI composite validator stack, updated parity counts (self-healing 3/10, overall 29/75), and validated with cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace, plus targeted scrub/parity tests. cargo fmt --check still reports broad pre-existing formatting drift in unrelated files.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","repair"]}
 {"id":"bd-vdk6","title":"External journal: compute journal segments from external device geometry","description":"When replaying an external journal, we need to compute JournalSegment entries. For an internal journal, segments come from the journal inode's extent tree. For an external journal, the entire device is the journal:
- JBD2 superblock occupies block 0.
- Journal blocks span from block s_first_log_block to block s_maxlen.
- Each journal block = one filesystem block (block size from JBD2 superblock).

Implement a function external_journal_segments(jbd2_sb: &Jbd2Superblock) -> Vec<JournalSegment> that produces the segment list for an external journal device.

ACCEPTANCE: Function correctly computes segments. Test with a synthetic JBD2 superblock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:42.033454065Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:38:08.307269936Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:38:08.306791541Z","close_reason":"Added Jbd2Superblock::external_journal_segment() that computes JournalSegment from first_log_block and max_len. Commit 7a8ede4.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","journal","journal_dev"]}
 {"id":"bd-vgu","title":"FUSE: Add fuser dependency + skeleton Filesystem impl delegating to FsOps","description":"Goal: make ffs-fuse a thin adapter: kernel FUSE requests -> internal FsOps.

Deliverables:
- Add fuser crate dependency (phase-gated if needed).
- Implement fuser::Filesystem with stubs that call into a boxed FsOps.
- Ensure all errors map through FfsError::to_errno().

Acceptance:
- cargo test passes with fuser integrated.
- The filesystem can be mounted in a no-op mode (returns ENOSYS for unimplemented ops).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:25.716873087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:03:51.276679325Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:03:51.276651664Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vgu","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:51.835670432Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vgu","depends_on_id":"bd-3is","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:51.923969054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-vhwh","title":"Support unrestricted FUSE ioctls for FIEMAP transport","description":"Investigate and implement a path for unrestricted FUSE ioctls so FS_IOC_FIEMAP reaches FrankenFS instead of being rejected in transport dispatch. Current evidence: fuser 0.16.0 returns ENOSYS/EOPNOTSUPP for unrestricted ioctls before the filesystem ioctl callback runs, so harness coverage currently soft-skips on that transport limitation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldSwan","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:56:59.317029990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T17:49:25.248104368Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T17:49:25.247841801Z","close_reason":"Investigated the FUSE FIEMAP transport path and found FS_IOC_FIEMAP does not reach FrankenFS's userspace ioctl handler in the current stack; no in-repo transport fix identified","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fiemap","fuse","transport"]}
 {"id":"bd-vql1","title":"External journal: wire replay through external device ByteDevice","description":"Wire the external journal replay path:
1. Open the external journal image as a FileByteDevice.
2. Read journal blocks from the external device.
3. Feed them to replay_jbd2_segments (existing function) with the DATA device as the write target.
4. After successful replay, clear needs_recovery on the data device.

The key insight: journal blocks are READ from the external device, but replayed blocks are WRITTEN to the data device. The existing replay infrastructure accepts a BlockDevice for writes — just pass the data device's block adapter.

DEPENDS ON: Journal segment computation (bd-vdk6).

ACCEPTANCE: A test with a synthetic data FS + dirty external journal verifies that replay writes land on the data device and needs_recovery is cleared.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:52.815573813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:41:58.713180412Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:41:58.712703279Z","close_reason":"Already implemented by concurrent agent: ExternalJournalReplayAdapter reads journal blocks from external device, writes replayed blocks to data device. UUID/block-size validation, JBD2 replay, superblock re-read after replay. Test open_fs_replays_external_journal_transaction passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","journal","journal_dev"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vql1","depends_on_id":"bd-vdk6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:02.571799150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vql1","depends_on_id":"bd-xvoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:35.150342179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-vyt6","title":"Add current-engine terminology erratum to comprehensive spec and fix ext4-only top-line wording","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md still opens with ext4-only wording and repeatedly uses FrankenFsEngine nomenclature that can be misread as the active FUSE runtime path. Add a focused errata bullet in §0.1.1 clarifying current code-path mapping () and adjust the top-line intro sentence to ext4+btrfs wording. Keep changes minimal and avoid broad spec rewrites.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T01:44:56.210135235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T01:45:18.135506890Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T01:45:18.135487854Z","close_reason":"Completed focused spec errata note for engine terminology and ext4+btrfs intro wording","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","spec"]}

 {"id":"bd-wpw7","title":"ffs-cli evidence command reads entire ledger into memory","description":"evidence_cmd currently loads the entire JSONL ledger with std::fs::read before parsing. Replace with line-stream parsing from BufReader, preserving torn-line skipping, event filtering, and tail behavior without whole-file buffering.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T19:39:31.217649389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T19:44:56.189599307Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T19:44:56.189576645Z","close_reason":"Completed: evidence_cmd now parses ledger via BufReader line streaming (no whole-file read), preserves malformed-line skip semantics, applies event-type filter in-stream, and tail mode uses bounded VecDeque. Added regression tests for filter/invalid-line behavior and filtered tail ordering; ran fmt, targeted tests, check, clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","memory","reliability"]}

All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xe2f","title":"Eliminate runtime panic on JBD2 failure after MVCC commit","description":"ffs-core journaled_commit currently panics if JBD2 commit fails after MVCC commit, causing hard process abort and state divergence handling via panic instead of explicit recovery/error mode. Implement deterministic non-panicking failure path with explicit degraded/read-only transition and tests.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:09:22.740373873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T18:25:05.924838587Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T18:25:05.924817308Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","journal","mvcc"],"comments":[{"id":284,"issue_id":"bd-xe2f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented non-panicking journaled commit path in ffs-core + mvcc preflight/apply split. JBD2 failure now returns FfsError before MVCC visibility and no longer panics. Added regression test journaled_commit_jbd2_failure_returns_error_without_visibility. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check/clippy workspace pass; targeted tests pass (new regression + ext4 journal replay tests).","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:25:00Z"}]}

 ## Root Cause Analysis
 
@@ -59,16 +59,18 @@ currently expose through the FUSE interface.
 - mremap is not part of the standard FUSE operation set.
 - **Path to fix**: Implement FUSE writeback cache + mmap support in ffs-fuse.
 
-**ext4/001 — ZERO_RANGE path exists, FIEMAP verification still missing**
-- FrankenFS now has a live ext4 `FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE` implementation in
+**ext4/001 — ZERO_RANGE + FIEMAP implemented (bd-pqpu)**
+- FrankenFS has a live ext4 `FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE` implementation in
   `ffs-core::ext4_fallocate`, and regression coverage exercises both data
   zeroing and `KEEP_SIZE` behavior.
-- The remaining xfstests blocker is FIEMAP-based verification of the resulting
-  extent layout. FrankenFS does not currently expose FIEMAP through the FUSE
-  surface, so the test cannot validate the post-zero-range mapping the way the
-  kernel ext4 path does.
-- **Path to fix**: Implement FIEMAP ioctl passthrough (or equivalent extent
-  inspection support consumable by the xfstests flow) in `ffs-fuse`.
+- FIEMAP ioctl passthrough is now implemented in `ffs-fuse` (bd-pqpu,
+  2026-03-31). The `FsOps::fiemap` trait method queries the ext4 extent tree
+  via `collect_extents_with_scope` and returns `FiemapExtent` entries with
+  proper `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST` and `FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN` flags. The FUSE
+  `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B), marshals the fiemap
+  header and extent array, and replies via `ReplyIoctl`.
+- **Status**: Reclassified from `known_fail` to `likely_pass`. Needs runtime
+  xfstests validation when the build environment is available.
 
 **ext4/003 — bigalloc scratch filesystem**
 - Requires creating a scratch ext4 filesystem with bigalloc feature enabled.
